meta.transcripts_note: The files /mnt/data/Kelly 5th grad cousin stories_original.txt and /mnt/data/Kelly mark desmond story_original.txt were not found in this workspace. Story language below matches the privacy-safe STAR cards in Kelly_Peterson_Resume_Portfolio.docx only.
flags: [NEEDS CONFIRMATION: professional email for resume, site, and mailto links]

summary: Special education teacher and university field supervisor with 26+ years across SDC, RSP/Inclusion, and general education (TK–8) in public and private schools.

Master’s-trained in special education (Chapman University, 2018) with a Clear Education Specialist Credential (Mild/Moderate); supports IEP-aligned instruction, MTSS/RTI, UDL, and inclusive collaboration.

Coaches and evaluates credential candidates for Chapman University (CalTPA aligned to California’s 13 TPEs), including program-level readiness recommendations for completion (approval/non-approval); known for steady partnership with students and families and durable literacy confidence growth.
signature_impact: Chapman University Field Supervisor (Special Education & Multiple Subject candidates): coaches and evaluates candidates during clinical practice and student teaching; supports CalTPA using the 13 TPEs; observes, conferences, and documents formative/summative feedback; collaborates with mentor teachers and program leadership; recommends completion readiness (approval/non-approval).

Mission Hills Christian School (Junior High English): supported a long-term shift from active reading avoidance to engagement across two school years; in high school the student independently chose novel-study coursework and credited the earlier confidence foundation as the reason they were willing to try.

Chino Unified School District (Anna Borba Elementary): supported two students who had been labeled as behavior concerns through relationship-first check-ins, barrier reduction, and interest-based engagement; strengthened family–school partnership and long-horizon student trajectories (privacy-safe details only).

Orange Unified School District (Olive Elementary): designs and delivers individualized, standards-based instruction for students with mild/moderate disabilities across SDC and RSP/Inclusion service models with MTSS/RTI and UDL-aligned practices.

Riley Elementary (Chino USD, volunteer): volunteered in a fifth-grade classroom; organized an entire grade-level Revolutionary War walk-through program; conducted weekly fifth-grade reading assessments and supported reading assessments in the Special Education department for intervention planning and progress monitoring.

Scope across settings: long-term elementary assignments (grades 1–6, including a combination class), junior high English, sixth-grade teaching, science fair coordination, and ACSI-related student academic competitions (as reflected in resume history).
core_competencies: IEP development and standards-based individualized instruction
MTSS/RTI, progress monitoring, and assessment administration
UDL, lesson planning, differentiation, and small-group instruction
Classroom management and positive behavior supports
Clinical supervision, observation, and written evaluation
CalTPA and TPE-aligned coaching for credential candidates

certifications.name: Clear Education Specialist Instructional Credential, Mild/Moderate
Multiple Subject Teaching Credential (Clear); renewal on file
certifications.date: Renewal: January 1, 2021 [NEEDS CONFIRMATION: confirm current renewal cycle on CTC printout]

experience.title: Education Specialist (SDC; RSP/Inclusion)
bullets: Develop and implement individualized, standards-based instruction aligned to IEP goals for students with mild/moderate disabilities across SDC and RSP/Inclusion service models.

Apply MTSS/RTI and UDL-aligned practices to plan measurable goals, deliver small-group and individualized instruction, monitor progress, administer assessments, and partner with general education teachers on inclusion supports.

Maintain proactive family communication; participate in IEP meetings, placement discussions, and required documentation; lead a structured classroom environment and direct assigned paraprofessionals; support positive behavior and safe participation; attend site/district special education meetings as needed.

experience.title: Field Supervisor, Special Education & Multiple Subject Credential Candidates
bullets: Supervise graduate candidates during clinical practice and student teaching; provide coaching and formal evaluation aligned to program standards and the 13 California Teaching Performance Expectations (TPEs).

Conduct observations and pre/post conferences; deliver documented formative and summative feedback; support CalTPA-related expectations across lesson design, differentiation, classroom management, assessment, and portfolio milestones; collaborate with mentor teachers and university program leadership; complete required documentation and participate in evaluation meetings.
Provide program-level readiness recommendations for credential advancement and completion (approval/non-approval).

experience.title: Special Education Instructional Aide
bullets: Supported students with disabilities in small-group and individual instruction under the direction of the special education classroom teacher; assisted with assignments, projects, and daily routines; supervised students during structured non-instructional activities as assigned.

experience.title: Volunteer — Fifth-Grade Classroom & Special Education Support
experience.date_note: Dates appear in Kelly_Peterson_Resume_Portfolio.docx as 2013–2016; not listed on the 2020 resume file. [NEEDS CONFIRMATION: exact months/years]
bullets: Volunteered in a fifth-grade classroom; organized and led an entire grade-level Revolutionary War walk-through program (materials, stations, and rotations).
Conducted weekly fifth-grade reading assessments and supported reading assessments in the Special Education department to inform intervention planning and progress monitoring.

experience.title: Junior High English Teacher
bullets: Taught junior high English with curriculum responsibilities and individualized academic planning for students with elevated learning needs; collaborated with the school psychologist on individualized programming (elementary and junior high).
Sponsored student participation in regional speech, spelling, and creative writing competitions through ACSI-related programs.

Reluctant reader growth (privacy-safe): supported a multi-year shift from reading avoidance to engagement; the student later chose high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation.
story_bullets: Reluctant reader growth (privacy-safe): supported a multi-year shift from reading avoidance to engagement; the student later chose high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation.

bullets: Delivered daily instruction across multiple grade levels (including a 1/2 combination class) with long-term assignments in fifth and sixth grades; fifth-grade Master Teacher and Grade Level Coordinator; collaborated with the resource specialist on inclusion-type instruction; RTI team; Specialized Academic & Behavior Intervention Team (West End SELPA trained); Conflict Managers training (West End SELPA program); science fair coordination; School Site Council; district textbook adoption; PQR team training; collaborative DARE instruction with the Chino Police Department; IB Early Primary Years training and certification.

High-need student support (privacy-safe): supported two students labeled as behavior concerns through relationship-first check-ins, barrier reduction, and interest-based engagement; strengthened family partnership and long-horizon trajectories (no identifying details).

impact_stories.title: Student outcomes and family partnership

star.situation: Two students were frequently regarded as behavior concerns, with low trust in school and uneven support outside of school affecting engagement.
star.task: Improve engagement and belonging while partnering with families in a respectful, sustainable way.
star.action: Used structured daily check-ins to build trust before raising academic demands; reduced participation barriers when signals indicated basic needs gaps; used interest-based strategies to strengthen self-efficacy and school connection.
star.result: Both students showed stronger engagement and participation; caregivers became active participants in school events after a long gap. Years later, both students described the steady support as important to avoiding negative outcomes and building stability.

impact_stories.star_card_word_count_note: Website STAR text is capped at 160 words total (privacy-safe; no names; no disability labels).

star_web.situation: At Anna Borba, two students were often seen as behavior concerns, with low trust in school and uneven support outside of school.
star_web.task: Improve engagement while partnering with families in a steady, respectful way.
star_web.action: Built trust through structured daily check-ins before raising academic demands; reduced participation barriers when basic-needs signals appeared; used interest-based strategies to strengthen confidence and connection.
star_web.result: Engagement and participation strengthened; caregivers began participating in school events after a long gap. Years later, both students described the consistent support as important to avoiding negative outcomes and building stability.

impact_stories.title: From reading avoidance to elective novel study

star.situation: A student arrived with strong reading avoidance; a caregiver communicated low expectations that the student would become a reader.
star.task: Rebuild confidence without turning reading into a high-stakes performance task on day one.
star.action: Prioritized scaffolded wins, visible early success, and a consistent “you can do this” stance across two school years rather than a remediation-only tone.
star.result: The student moved from avoidance to genuine engagement and self-directed reading interest; later enrolled in high school novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation as the reason they were willing to try.

impact_stories.star_card_word_count_note: Website STAR text is capped at 160 words total (privacy-safe; no names; no disability labels).

star_web.situation: In junior high English, a student arrived with strong reading avoidance, and a caregiver communicated low expectations that the student would become a reader.
star_web.task: Rebuild confidence without making early reading feel like a high-stakes performance task.
star_web.action: Used scaffolded wins and visible early success across two school years, with a consistent strengths-based stance rather than a remediation-only tone.
star_web.result: The student moved from avoidance toward engagement and self-directed reading interest; in high school, the student chose novel-study electives and credited the earlier confidence foundation as the reason they were willing to try.
